content.problemStatement:
A sequence of integers (a1,a2,Рђд,aka_1, a_2, \ldots, a_ka1РђІ,a2РђІ,Рђд,akРђІ) is said to be UpDown, if these inequalities hold true:


a1РЅцa2a_1 \le a_2a1РђІРЅцa2РђІ
a2РЅЦa3a_2 \ge a_3a2РђІРЅЦa3РђІ
a3РЅцa4a_3 \le a_4a3РђІРЅцa4РђІ
and so on.
That is, every even-indexed element should be at least as large as its adjacent elements. And every odd-indexed element should be at most as large as its adjacent elements. Formally, a2iРЅЦa2i+1a_{2i} \ge a_{2i+1}a2iРђІРЅЦa2i+1РђІ and a2i+1РЅцa2i+2a_{2i+1} \le a_{2i+2}a2i+1РђІРЅцa2i+2РђІ, for all valid positions.

A subsegment is a consecutive portion of a sequence. That is, a subsegment of (b1,b2,Рђд,bkb_1, b_2, \ldots, b_kb1РђІ,b2РђІ,Рђд,bkРђІ)  will be of the form (bi,bi+1,Рђд,bjb_i, b_{i+1}, \ldots, b_jbiРђІ,bi+1РђІ,Рђд,bjРђІ), for some iii and jjj.

You are given a sequence (s1,s2,Рђд,sns_1, s_2, \ldots, s_ns1РђІ,s2РђІ,Рђд,snРђІ). You can insert at most one integer anywhere in this sequence. It could be any integer. After inserting an integer (or choosing not to), suppose you have the new sequence (t1,t2,Рђд,tmt_1, t_2, \ldots, t_mt1РђІ,t2РђІ,Рђд,tmРђІ). Note that mmm will either be nnn+1 or nnn. You want to maximize the length of the longest subsegment of (t1,t2,Рђд,tmt_1, t_2, \ldots, t_mt1РђІ,t2РђІ,Рђд,tmРђІ) which is UpDown, and output the length of that.

###Input


The first line contains a single integer, TTT, which is the number of testcases. The description of each testcase follows.
The first line of every testcase contains a single integer, nnn, which is the number of integers in the original sequence.
The second line contains nnn integers: s1,s2,Рђд,sns_1, s_2, \ldots, s_ns1РђІ,s2РђІ,Рђд,snРђІ, which forms the original sequence.
###Output
For each testcase output a single line containing one integer, which should be the length of the longest UpDown subsegment that you can get after inserting at most one integer.

###Constraints


1РЅцTРЅц21 \le T \le 21РЅцTРЅц2
1РЅцnРЅц1061 \le n \le 10^61РЅцnРЅц106
1РЅцsiРЅц1091 \le s_i \le 10^91РЅцsiРђІРЅц109
###Subtasks

**Subtask #1 (20 points):** 1РЅцnРЅц1001 \le n \le 1001РЅцnРЅц100

**Subtask #2 (10 points):** 1РЅцnРЅц100001 \le n \le 100001РЅцnРЅц10000
constraints: **Testcase 1:** The original sequence is (100, 1, 10, 3, 20, 25, 24). Suppose you insert the element 5 between the elements 20 and 25, you will get the new sequence (100, 1, 10, 3, 20, 5, 25, 24). The longest UpDown subsegment of this sequence is (1, 10, 3, 20, 5, 25, 24), whose length is 7. You can check that you cannot do any better, and hence the answer is 7.

**Testcase 2:** The original sequence is (3, 3, 2, 4, 1). Suppose you insert the element 4 at the end, you will get the new sequence (3, 3, 2, 4, 1, 4). This entire sequence is UpDown, and so the longest UpDown subsegment of this sequence is (3, 3, 2, 4, 1, 4), whose length is 6. You can check that you cannot do any better, and hence the answer is 6.
